title: Conventional commits
outcome: Lightweight conventional-commit convention adopted (feat|fix|docs|chore|refactor|test). Dependency-free bash commit-msg hook (packages/archon/hooks/commit-msg, regex, no commitlint/cog/czg) wired into the pre-commit config at the commit-msg stage; merge/revert/fixup subjects pass through. docs/git-hygiene.md records the grammar + the lightweight-vs-enforced decision.
validation: commit-msg hook accepts a conforming subject (exit 0) and rejects a non-conforming one with a helpful message (exit 1). shellcheck clean; validate-config passes.
decisions: lightweight now; commitlint/cog/czg deferred until history-based automation (changelog/release notes) needs it — none installed, installs human-gated
token_note: measured rtk gain on this host: git status ~63% (38→14 tok), git log --oneline -30 ~11% (303→270 tok), git log -3 -p ~99% (27k→200 tok). Grammar-consistent conventional subjects keep oneline history RTK-compressible; see docs/git-hygiene.md#token-cost-measured
